seoTitle: Knights Hill vs Knockrow — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Knights Hill and Knockrow. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Knights Hill vs Knockrow: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Knights Hill and Knockrow.
heading: Knights Hill vs Knockrow — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Knights Hill, New South Wales and Knockrow, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Knights Hill has a lower overall climate risk score (39/100) compared to Knockrow (44/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Knights Hill or Knockrow safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Knights Hill has a flood risk score of 24/100, while Knockrow scores 52/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Knights Hill scores 47/100 for bushfire risk. Knockrow scores 61/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Knights Hill or Knockrow?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Knights Hill has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.15% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
